agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond to add caste as protected category in city code
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a resolution and first-reading ordinance to add caste as a protected category in the Richmond Municipal Code, amending sections 3.58.010, 3.58.020, and 3.58.060. If approved, caste-based discrimination would be treated the same as discrimination based on race, religion, or gender under the Human Rights and Human Relations Commission. The HRHR Commission unanimously recommended adoption on January 26, 2026. Modest costs for staff training and reporting mechanisms would be absorbed into existing DEI budgets.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments if you want to have a say on this proposed change.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
O.1.a
Establishing a Commitment to Caste Equity and the Prevention of Caste-Based Discrimination
ADOPT a resolution establishing a Commitment to Caste Equity and the Prevention of Caste-Based Discrimination and INTRODUCE an ordinance (first reading) amending sections 3.58.010, 3.58.020, and 3.58.060 of the Richmond Municipal Code to add caste as a protected category within the jurisdiction of the City’s Human Rights and Human Relations Commission – City Attorney’s Office (Shannon Moore 510-620-6505/Dallae Chin 510-620-5517/Brandi Robinson 510-620-5518).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond to limit city resources for federal immigration enforcement
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on second reading of an ordinance amending RMC Chapter 2.30 to prohibit use of city property as staging areas, processing locations, or operational bases for federal civil immigration enforcement. The ordinance also requires staff training, a community communications plan, and reporting requirements. If approved, it takes effect May 28, 2026.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote on April 28, 2026 — if passed, the ordinance takes effect May 28, 2026.

Ordinance takes effect — Richmond immigration enforcement limits

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: City-wide records retention schedule updates up for council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on a resolution to adopt updated city-wide records retention and destruction schedules (excluding police). The new schedules set clear retention periods for all city records (permits, licences, financial documents, emails, etc.) and authorize destruction after the period expires with written consent from the department head, city clerk, and city attorney. agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a resolution directing the city clerk to send a letter to California Governor Gavin Newsom supporting a joint task force of fire and law enforcement agencies from California and Nevada, and an interstate compact requiring Nevada fireworks retailers to download sales records into a secure state website accessible to fire/law enforcement agencies and ATF. The letter cites 13 Nevada stores (6 in Pahrump, 7 northeast of Reno) accounting for 65-75% of illegal fireworks used in California, and notes over 600,000 pounds of illegal fireworks were seized last year.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to support California fire agency bill
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council will consider a resolution supporting California Senate Bill 959 (Grayson/Arreguín). The bill would amend Education Code Section 46392 to allow a local fire agency to determine the imminence of a major safety hazard during extreme wildfire emergencies. If a school district, charter school, or county office of education closes or attendance drops due to that determination, state funding tied to Average Daily Attendance (ADA) would be protected. The resolution directs the city clerk to send copies to Governor Newsom, Senators Grayson and Arreguín, Assemblymember Wicks, the California Fire Chiefs Association, and the West Contra Costa Unified School District Board.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to consider moratorium on data centres
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council will vote on a proposal to direct the city manager and city attorney to work with planning staff to develop a proposed moratorium on new data centres. The moratorium would temporarily pause approvals while the city studies impacts on the electrical grid, water use, land use, noise, and the environment. agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider update on Richmond's Sanctuary City contracting policy
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council received a written update on compliance with Ordinance No. 12-18 N.S., which prohibits the city from contracting with any person or entity that provides Data Broker or Extreme Vetting services to ICE. All contractors must submit a Sanctuary City Compliance Statement with their bid or proposal before contract execution. Violations constitute material default, may result in contract termination and a five-year ban from bidding, and knowingly supplying false information is a misdemeanor subject to a $1,000 fine.
